articles.title: Starting to see signs of middle- and lower-income consumers getting squeezed: Liz Everett Krisberg
articles.excerpt: Liz Everett Krisberg, head of Bank of America Institute, joins ‘Squawk Box' to break down the Institute's Consumer Checkpoint Report, which provides real-time consumer spending and financial health estimates.

articles.title: Inflation Report Today: Dow Futures Fall, Oil Rises Amid Mideast Stalemate
articles.excerpt: The tech-led rally is taking a breather as investors wait for inflation data. U.S. stock futures are trading lower, with contracts pegged to the Nasdaq 100 falling the most.

articles.title: Urals crude premiums ease in India as weak margins curb buying
articles.excerpt: Premiums for Russia's Urals crude oil in India have declined as refiners cut purchases amid weak ​refining margins, three traders said.

articles.title: Trump is taking more than a dozen U.S. executives to China. Jensen Huang isn't one of them
articles.excerpt: U.S. President Donald Trump is meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping later this week in Beijing. The White House has asked more than a dozen U.S. executives to join Trump's trip, including Qualcomm's Cristiano Amon.

articles.title: Sell In May And Warsh Away
articles.excerpt: Kevin Warsh's appointment as Federal Reserve chairman signals a shift toward tighter monetary policy and constrained government intervention. Warsh's background and Hoover Institution ties suggest a preference for shrinking the Fed balance sheet and limiting QE, raising systemic

articles.title: An AI Windfall Tax? The Mere Mention Just Derailed the World's Hottest Stock Market.
articles.excerpt: South Korea's KOSPI index took a tumble Monday as an official floated the idea of a giving excess AI profits to the country's citizens.

articles.title: Private credit funds slash loan values as borrower stress rises
articles.excerpt: Private credit funds have marked down more than a tenth of ​their loans by at least 50%, new data from MSCI ‌showed, as corporate borrowers in this $3.5 trillion market struggle with growing debt burdens.

articles.title: Trump-Xi expected to discuss AI safety as China narrows tech gap with U.S. models
articles.excerpt: Artificial intelligence safety is expected to be on the agenda at this week's meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing. CNBC's Evelyn Cheng reports from Beijing.

articles.title: China's US ethane imports hit record as Iran war cut rival feedstock supply
articles.excerpt: China boosted U.S. ethane imports to all-time highs in April to capitalise on strong ​petrochemical margins as the supply of other feedstocks from the Middle East was disrupted by the Iran ‌war, according to traders, analysts and shipping data.

articles.title: This Chip Stocks Rally Is Suddenly Fragile. Why Markets Should Worry.
articles.excerpt: Trump's China summit threatens the AI boom, Ford gets into the energy storage business, Hims & Hers posts a surprise loss, and more news to start your day.

articles.title: Norway's Hammerfest LNG repairs broken valve after shutdown
articles.excerpt: Norway's Hammerfest LNG export terminal is undergoing ​repairs for a mechanical fault ‌in a valve that halted production last week, a spokesperson ​for operator Equinor said ​on Tuesday.

articles.title: India's inflation in April rises for sixth straight month, but undershoots estimates
articles.excerpt: The RBI warned Middle East tensions could hurt growth and fuel inflation pressures. The rupee hovered near record lows as higher energy costs are expected to significantly widen the country's trade and current account deficits.

articles.title: Don't Fight The Tape: The Rally Is Narrow For A Reason

articles.title: Trump readies for Beijing summit with Xi as AI chip sales, farm goods top agenda
articles.excerpt: President Trump travels to China for a summit with Xi Jinping as trade tensions, AI chip restrictions and the Iran war reshape U.S.-China relations.

articles.title: US CEOs seek China business gains from Trump-Xi summit
articles.excerpt: More than a dozen CEOs and top executives will accompany Trump on his visit.

articles.title: Despite the War, Energy Stocks Are Cheap
articles.excerpt: In the midst of an oil crisis, the biggest bargains can be found in energy stocks.

articles.title: The hottest stock market in the world finally met its match: taxes
articles.excerpt: The idea that citizens should get a cut of the booming profits of tech companies derailed one of the hottest stock markets in the world on Tuesday.

articles.title: This dot-com survivor says AI buildout is more like 1997 than 1999 — and still urges investors to have more cash
articles.excerpt: The riveting gains for microchip stocks have many analysts drawing parallels to 1999, just ahead of the dot-com crash, when the build-out of a key technology led to parabolic moves and frenzied speculation.

articles.title: Nikkei 225 Bullish Run Is Facing Minor Exhaustion Below 64,145
